mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in retail sales (RSAFS) indicates robust consumer demand, which translates directly into higher corporate revenues and earnings, thereby driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon as investor sentiment improves.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 55% of periods where RSAFS shows positive month-over-month growth.

mechanism_narrative: A significant and rapid increase in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) signals a deteriorating labor market, which typically precedes a broader economic slowdown and reduced consumer spending, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns are positive in more than 40% of instances where UNRATE rises by more than 0.5 percentage points over a three-month period.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation (CPIAUCSL) is elevated and the Federal Funds Rate (FEDFUNDS) is also high, it indicates a tight monetary policy environment aimed at curbing price pressures. This combination typically dampens economic growth and compresses equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns are positive in more than 50% of periods where both CPIAUCSL is above 250 and FEDFUNDS is above 1.5.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of robust industrial production growth (INDPRO), indicating strong supply-side activity and manufacturing health, equity markets tend to react positively to continued expansion, reflecting optimism about future corporate profits and economic momentum over a 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns are negative in more than 50% of instances where INDPRO shows strong 3-month annualized growth during an industrial expansion regime.

mechanism_narrative: A significant and sustained decline in housing starts (HOUST) signals weakening demand in the housing sector, which is a leading indicator of broader economic slowdown and reduced consumer confidence, negatively impacting equity markets over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns are positive in more than 45% of periods when the HOUST 3-month average drops below 1200 thousand units.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained job creation, as indicated by strong non-farm payrolls (PAYEMS), reflects a healthy labor market and robust consumer spending capacity. This supports corporate revenues and drives positive equity market performance over a 20-day horizon as investors anticipate continued economic strength.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns are negative in more than 50% of periods where PAYEMS 3-month average change exceeds 150,000 persons.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent high inflation (CPIAUCSL) erodes purchasing power and corporate profit margins, leading to investor concerns about future earnings and potential central bank tightening. This results in negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon as risk premiums expand.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns are positive in more than 40% of periods when CPIAUCSL 12-month change exceeds 3%.

mechanism_narrative: When GDP growth (GDPC1) is strong but the Federal Funds Rate (FEDFUNDS) remains low, it suggests an accommodative monetary policy supporting a growing economy. This combination is highly favorable for equity market performance, driving positive S&P 500 returns over a 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns are negative in more than 50% of periods where GDPC1 annualized growth is strong and FEDFUNDS is low.
